rationale: No new qualitative developments affecting Romania directly; Iran deal momentum sustains energy price optimism, and Black Sea/Ukraine situation unchanged.

top_events.title: Iran signals Hormuz reopening tied to peace deal
top_events.summary: Continued momentum toward an Iran deal could lower energy costs for Romania, which remains sensitive to gas and oil prices. Deal still unsigned — no qualitative change from prior cycle.

top_events.title: Trump seeks 'off-ramp' from Iran war
top_events.summary: US diplomatic push toward Iran deal reduces risk of broader Middle East escalation, indirectly supporting energy price stability relevant to Romanian households.

top_events.title: Israel hits south Lebanon, issues displacement orders
top_events.summary: Israeli strikes in Lebanon signal continued Middle East instability, but no direct spillover to Romania's Black Sea region or economy this cycle.
